summary: Elmwood Home Care, a home healthcare provider serving Rhode Island and Massachusetts, disclosed a cybersecurity incident involving unauthorized access to its systems between 2026-01-24 and 2026-02-13. Forensic investigation determined a threat group viewed or acquired patient files containing names, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, driver's license numbers, other demographic data, medical information, and health insurance information. The LockBit 5.0 (LockBit5) ransomware group claimed responsibility and threatened to publish the stolen data on the dark web (leak-site post dated 2026-0

Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk (maker of Ozempic and Wegovy) disclosed that attackers gained unauthorized access to internal IT systems and copied non-public data, including pseudonymized clinical-trial patient data (patient IDs, trial participation, sex, birth year, biomarkers, health/immunogenicity data, lifestyle factors), healthcare-provider contact details, internal AI model assets and other proprietary data. The hacking group FulcrumSec claimed responsibility, saying it entered via a GitHub access token in March 2026, dwelled for 2+ months, and exfiltrated a claimed 1.3TB. Afte
